evidence: 12 commits with clear progression: chore → feat:tools → feat:detectives → feat:judges → feat:justice
Conventional commit format followed throughout (feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:)
Some commit clustering detected — multiple feat: commits within short time windows
git_forensic_analysis.notes: Rule of Functionality applied. Tech Lead confirmed history meets production standard. Minor clustering noted but not a critical violation.

evidence: Pydantic BaseModel classes: Evidence, JudicialOpinion, CriterionResult, AuditReport
AgentState uses TypedDict with Annotated[Dict, operator.ior] for evidences
AgentState uses Annotated[List, operator.add] for opinions — parallel-safe
Field constraints: score ge=1 le=5, confidence ge=0.0 le=1.0
Docstring explicitly explains race condition each reducer prevents
state_management_rigor.notes: Unanimous 5/5. Production-grade state management with correct reducer choices and validation constraints.

evidence: Two fan-out/fan-in patterns: detective layer and judicial layer
add_conditional_edges() with explicit mapping dict for error routing
judicial_fanout_node intermediate pass-through adds indirection
operator.ior and operator.add reducers ensure parallel write safety
Graph confirmed running end-to-end producing real audit artifacts
graph_orchestration_architecture.notes: Rule of Functionality applied. Graph runs correctly. Intermediate fanout node is valid but could be replaced with Send API for cleaner dispatch.

evidence: tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() with try/finally — cleanup guaranteed even on exception
subprocess.run() exclusively — no os.system() calls anywhere
URL allowlist: only github.com and gitlab.com accepted
Timeouts set: 120s clone, 30s git log
AST walking (ast.walk) for graph analysis — not regex
safe_tool_engineering.notes: Unanimous 5/5. Production-grade sandboxing with no security violations found.

evidence: All three judges use .with_structured_output(JudicialOpinion)
_invoke_with_retry() provides two-attempt fallback with valid JudicialOpinion on failure
Lazy _get_llm() prevents import-time crash when API key not loaded
_get_llm() creates new ChatAnthropic instance per criterion — 30 instantiations per run
structured_output_enforcement.notes: Structured output correctly enforced. LLM instantiation inefficiency is technical debt. functools.lru_cache would reduce to 1 instantiation per run.

evidence: Prosecutor: 'Trust No One. Assume Vibe Coding.' — adversarial, charges specific crimes
Defense: 'Reward Effort and Intent. Look for Spirit of the Law.' — generous interpretation
Tech Lead: 'Does it actually work? Is it maintainable?' — pragmatic empirical
Generated audit report confirms genuinely different scores per judge per dimension
All three share bullet-format prompt structure — reasoning style not fundamentally distinct
judicial_nuance_and_dialectics.notes: Rule of Functionality applied. Personas produce genuinely different scores confirmed by generated output. Prompt structure similarity noted.

evidence: _rule_of_security(): caps score at 3 on confirmed vulnerability regardless of Defense
_rule_of_evidence(): overrides Defense if detective found=False across all evidence
_rule_of_functionality(): weights TechLead 50%, others 25% for architecture criteria
Zero LLM calls in synthesis path — fully deterministic Python
Dissent path (_requires_dissent, variance > 2) present but not triggered in generated report
chief_justice_synthesis_engine.notes: Production-grade synthesis engine. Dissent path untested. architecture_criteria hardcoded as Python set rather than loaded from rubric.json.
